title: Ethereum mainnet may get a lot more room after Glamsterdam
summary:
1. Hasu pointed out something pretty under-discussed: after Glamsterdam, Ethereum’s gas limit could go from around 60M to roughly 200M.
2. What’s interesting is that this is not just “raise the limit and hope nodes survive” — ePBS gives payloads more time, BALs help clients prefetch/parallelize execution work, and gas repricing is supposed to keep state growth from getting reckless.
3. If demand does not grow at the same pace, L1 fees could stay very low for a while.
author: u/Enough_Angle_7839

title: Stables CEO Says Migrant Flows Favor USDT, Driving 60% Cross-Border Dollar Demand
summary:
1. Bernardo Bilotta argues that banks avoid stablecoins not due to a lack of technical understanding, but to protect their vital relationships with central banks and Western correspondent banks, who are notoriously risk-averse.
2. Key Takeaways: Bernardo Bilotta notes Asia handles 50% of global stablecoin flows, but banks fear regulatory risk.

title: Academy Board Bans AI Content at 99th Oscars, Forcing Human-Only Wins
summary:
1. The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences has established strict new regulations for the 99th Oscars, officially banning AI-generated content from top categories to protect human creativity.
2. Key Takeaways: Academy Board bans AI for the 99th Oscars, requiring human-only billing for acting and writing by March 2027.

title: Starlink Usage Turns Fatal in Iran Internet Blackout
summary:
1. As the digital blockade imposed by the Iranian regime reaches its tenth week, reports indicate that an individual has allegedly died for using Starlink devices to circumvent it.
2. Hesam Alaeddin, a 40-year-old man, was reportedly beaten to death for this transgression.
3. Key Takeaways: Iran’s 64-day digital blockade led to the death of Hesam Alaeddin for […]
author: Sergio Goschenko

title: Bitcoin logs best monthly performance in 12 months during April
summary: 1. Despite being the best-performing month in the past 12 months, it still came in slightly below its historical average, according to CoinGlass data.
